text: Hello.
zh: 大家好。

text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语学习台的六分钟英语节目。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Bekka.
zh: 我是贝卡。

text: Worrying about work or exams?
zh: 在为工作或考试发愁吗?

text: War and climate change in the news?
zh: 新闻里的战争和气候变化让你烦心吗?

text: It's no wonder that modern life is stressful.
zh: 难怪现代生活压力这么大。

text: What makes you stress, Neil?
zh: 尼尔,是什么让你感到压力?

text: Oh, good question.
zh: 哦,好问题。

text: I think not having enough time to do something.
zh: 我觉得是没有足够的时间去做某件事。

text: That really makes me stressed out.
zh: 这真的会让我感到压力很大。

text: How about you?
zh: 你呢?

text: When I'm trying to do something that I've never tried before and I'm not very good at it, that makes me feel really stressed.
zh: 当我尝试做一些从未做过、也不太擅长的事情时,我会感到非常有压力。

text: I find that stressful too.
zh: 我也觉得那样很有压力。

text: And how do you cope with stressful situations, Neil?
zh: 那你是怎么应对压力的呢,尼尔?

text: Well, I try to slow down, pause, take a breath and think that at some point, probably later today, everything will be OK again.
zh: 嗯,我会试着放慢脚步,停下来,深呼吸,然后想着到了某个时候,可能就是今天晚些时候,一切都会好起来的。

text: How about you?
zh: 你呢?

text: Yeah, same.
zh: 是啊,我也差不多。

text: But in my mind, I'm telling myself that I will be better at what I'm doing, I just need to practice more.
zh: 不过我心里会告诉自己,我会做得越来越好的,只是需要多加练习。

text: Hmm.
zh: 嗯。

text: Many young people who feel stressed about the modern world use the word overwhelm to describe their feelings.
zh: 很多对现代世界感到有压力的年轻人,会用“overwhelm(不堪重负)”这个词来形容自己的感受。

text: And overwhelm was also the topic of a recent BBC World Service programme What in the World?
zh: 而“overwhelm”也是BBC世界服务节目《What in the World?》最近一期的主题。

text: Here's Ikra Farooq and Claudia Hammond.
zh: 下面请听Ikra Farooq和Claudia Hammond的对话。

text: So overwhelm is an emotional state where you are struggling with your current situation.
zh: 所谓“不堪重负”,是一种情绪状态,指你正在为当前的处境苦苦挣扎。

text: So you might feel overloaded, you might feel you can't cope.
zh: 你可能会感到负担过重,觉得自己应付不来。

text: And that might be a temporary thing, hopefully, but if it's not in the longer term, if that becomes more of a kind of chronic kind of stress, then that might lead to burnout in the end.
zh: 希望这只是暂时的,但如果长期如此,变成一种慢性压力,最终可能导致“职业倦怠”。

text: Feeling overwhelmed and unable to cope with life can become chronic, meaning that it lasts for a long time.
zh: 感到不堪重负、无法应对生活,可能会变成慢性的,也就是说会持续很长时间。

text: Overwhelm can lead to burnout, mental or physical exhaustion caused by working too hard.
zh: “不堪重负”可能导致“职业倦怠”,也就是由于工作过度而造成的身心疲惫。

text: So, how can we manage the stress in our lives and feel happier and healthier?
zh: 那么,我们该如何管理生活中的压力,让自己更快乐、更健康呢?

text: That's what we'll be discussing in this episode, along with some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 这就是本期节目要讨论的内容,同时还会介绍一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: phrases.
zh: 短语。

text: You'll find all the vocabulary from this episode on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期节目的所有词汇。

text: But before we start, I have a question for you, Nil.
zh: 不过在开始之前,尼尔,我有个问题要问你。

text: One simple way of combating stress is taking time to rest.
zh: 缓解压力的一个简单方法就是抽时间休息。

text: So, according to research conducted by BBC presenter and psychologist Claudia Hammond, which activity is thought to be the most restful?
zh: 那么,根据BBC主持人兼心理学家Claudia Hammond所做的研究,哪种活动被认为是最能让人放松的?

text: Is it a reading for pleasure, b walking in nature, or c listening to music?
zh: 是a. 为兴趣而阅读,b. 在大自然中散步,还是c. 听音乐?

text: Well, I think they're all quite relaxing, but I'm going to guess b walking in nature.
zh: 嗯,我觉得这些都挺让人放松的,但我要猜b,在大自然中散步。

text: I'm surprised, Neil.
zh: 我很惊讶,尼尔。

text: I know you love music.
zh: 我知道你很喜欢音乐。

text: And we'll find out the answer later.
zh: 我们稍后会揭晓答案。

text: One of the most stressful things facing many young people is exams.
zh: 对许多年轻人来说,考试是最让人有压力的事情之一。

text: Here, Claudia Hammond offers some advice for managing exam stress to BBC World Service's What in the World?
zh: 下面,Claudia Hammond在BBC世界服务节目《What in the World?》中,就如何应对考试压力给出了一些建议。

text: Particularly in the exam itself if you feel stressed, then just knowing that you may well perform better while you're stressed,
zh: 尤其是在考试当中,如果你感到有压力,那么只要知道压力大的时候你很可能表现得更好,

text: that this concentrates the mind, it sharpens, you know, nerves sharpen your thinking and allow you to really do your best.
zh: 因为压力会让你集中注意力,让神经变得敏锐,从而让你的思维更清晰,发挥出最佳水平。

text: It's normal to feel nerves before an exam.
zh: 考试前感到紧张是很正常的。

text: Nerves is an informal word for worry or anxiety, but according to Claudia, nerves aren't necessarily bad.
zh: “nerves”是一个非正式用词,指担忧或焦虑,不过根据Claudia的说法,紧张并不一定是坏事。

text: Yes, nerves can help concentrate the mind.
zh: 没错,紧张感可以帮助集中注意力。

text: The phrase concentrate or sharpen the mind means to think more clearly about something.
zh: “concentrate”或“sharpen the mind”这个说法,意思是让思维对某事更加清晰。

text: Like an actor or an athlete, feeling nerves before a big performance can help them do their best.
zh: 就像演员或运动员一样,在重要表演前感到紧张,反而能帮助他们发挥出最佳水平。

text: Claudia recommends other ways to reduce stress as well.
zh: Claudia还推荐了其他一些减压的方法。

text: These include slowing down and accepting that it's OK to be good enough, rather than always trying for perfection.
zh: 其中包括放慢节奏,接受“做得足够好就行”,而不是一味追求完美。

text: Here she shares Here's more tips with Iqra Farooq of BBC World Service's What In The World.
zh: 下面她与BBC世界服务《What in the World?》的Iqra Farooq分享了更多建议。

text: And I guess there's no one size fits all as well when it comes to this.
zh: 我想在这方面也不存在一种放之四海而皆准的办法。

text: So if someone's listening and thinking, I want some top tips on just feeling overwhelmed generally in my life, what would you say to them?
zh: 如果有听众在想,我想要一些应对整体生活中不堪重负感的实用建议,你会对他们说什么呢?

text: So first I would say, accept that your to -do list is never going to end.
zh: 首先,我会说,要接受你的待办事项清单永远不会结束。

text: It is always going to be there.
zh: 它会一直存在。

text: So even, I know that might sound defeatist, but even if you get to the end of it today, tomorrow there will be something else on it.
zh: 我知道这听起来可能有点消极,但即使你今天把清单上的事都做完了,明天又会有新的事情加进去。

text: And that is just life.
zh: 这就是生活。

text: And that is just signs of a busy life.
zh: 这只是生活忙碌的一种表现罢了。

text: There are many ways to combat stress.
zh: 应对压力的方法有很多。

text: Not a one -size -fits -all solution.
zh: 并不存在一种放之四海而皆准的解决方案。

text: The phrase one -size -fits -all describes trying to apply the same solution to many different problems.
zh: “one-size-fits-all”这个说法,形容试图用同一种方法去解决许多不同的问题。

text: Claudia's final piece of advice is don't worry too much about your to -do list, the list of all the items you have to do.
zh: Claudia最后给出的建议是:不要过于担心你的待办事项清单,也就是你需要完成的所有事项列表。

text: Why?
zh: 为什么呢?

text: Because it never ends.
zh: 因为它永远不会结束。

text: Every day there will be something new to add to the list, so why stress?
zh: 每天都会有新的事情加进清单里,那又何必为此焦虑呢?

text: Some good advice there, Neil.
zh: 尼尔,这些建议真不错。

text: I think I'll try these techniques next time I feel stressed.
zh: 下次我感到有压力的时候,会试试这些方法。

text: And speaking of ways to de -stress reminds me of my question.
zh: 说到缓解压力的方法,这让我想起了我之前的问题。

text: I asked you earlier, according to research conducted by BBC presenter and psychologist Claudia Hammond, which activity is thought of to be the most restful?
zh: 我之前问你,根据BBC主持人兼心理学家Claudia Hammond所做的研究,哪种活动被认为是最能让人放松的?

text: And you answered b walking in nature.
zh: 你回答的是b,在大自然中散步。

text: Unfortunately Neil, the answer is a reading.
zh: 很遗憾,尼尔,正确答案是a,阅读。

text: Ah, well reading is really relaxing as well.
zh: 啊,不过阅读确实也很让人放松。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary from this episode, beginning with the adjective chronic, which means continuing for a long time.
zh: 好了,我们来回顾一下本期节目的词汇,首先是形容词“chronic”,意思是持续很长时间的。

text: Burnout is a feeling of physical or mental exhaustion caused by working too hard.
zh: “burnout”指由于工作过度而导致的身心疲惫感。

text: If you say that a difficult situation sharpens or concentrates someone's mind, you mean that it makes them think clearly.
zh: 如果你说某种困难的处境“sharpens”或“concentrates”一个人的思维,意思是它让人思路更加清晰。

text: Nerves are an informal word for worry or anxiety.
zh: “nerves”是一个非正式用词,指担忧或焦虑。

text: A one -size -fits -all approach tries to apply the same solution to all problems, rather than solving them individually.
zh: “one-size-fits-all”的做法,是试图用同一种解决方案应对所有问题,而不是逐一针对性解决。

text: And finally, a to -do list is a list of all the tasks you have to do.
zh: 最后,“to-do list”指你需要完成的所有任务清单。

text: Of course, another way to beat stress is doing something you love.
zh: 当然,战胜压力的另一个办法,就是去做你热爱的事情。

text: And if that's improving your English, then why not visit our website bbclearningenglish .com, where you'll find loads more activities for doing just that.
zh: 如果这件事是提高你的英语水平,那不妨访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,那里有大量相关活动供你练习。

text: See you again next time, but for now it's goodbye.
zh: 我们下次再见,现在先说再见啦。

text: Bye!
zh: 拜拜!

text: Six Minute English from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 六分钟英语,来自bbclearningenglish.com。
